featureChoices.name: Dragon Ancestor
featureChoices.description: Choose the type of dragon in your bloodline; it determines your draconic damage affinity.

options.name: Black dragon
options.description: Affinity: acid damage.

options.name: Blue dragon
options.description: Affinity: lightning damage.

options.name: Brass dragon
options.description: Affinity: fire damage.

options.name: Bronze dragon
options.description: Affinity: lightning damage.

options.name: Copper dragon
options.description: Affinity: acid damage.

options.name: Gold dragon
options.description: Affinity: fire damage.

options.name: Green dragon
options.description: Affinity: poison damage.

options.name: Red dragon
options.description: Affinity: fire damage.

options.name: Silver dragon
options.description: Affinity: cold damage.

options.name: White dragon
options.description: Affinity: cold damage.

features.name: Dragon Ancestor
features.description: Choose a dragon type as your ancestor. You speak, read, and write Draconic, and double your proficiency bonus on Charisma checks when interacting with dragons.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1 per sorcerer level. While you aren't wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: When you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your draconic ancestry, add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll. You can also spend 1 sorcery point to gain resistance to that damage type for an hour.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: As a bonus action, sprout dragon wings that give you a flying speed equal to your current speed (unusable in armor not made to accommodate them).

features.name: Draconic Presence
features.description: As an action, spend 5 sorcery points to exude an aura of awe or fear to 60 feet for 1 minute: hostile creatures that start their turn in it must succeed on a Wisdom save or be charmed or frightened.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You cast sorcerer spells using Charisma from a fixed list of spells known, and can use an arcane focus.

features.name: Sorcerous Origin
features.description: Your origin (chosen at 1st level) grants features at 1st, 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

features.name: Font of Magic
features.description: You have sorcery points equal to your sorcerer level (regained on a long rest). As a bonus action, convert sorcery points into spell slots or spell slots into sorcery points.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You know two Metamagic options (Careful, Distant, Empowered, Extended, Heightened, Quickened, Subtle, or Twinned Spell) and can spend sorcery points to shape your spells.

features.name: Sorcerous Restoration
features.description: You regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.

featureChoices.name: Metamagic
featureChoices.description: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs: choose two Metamagic options.

options.name: Careful Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, you spend 1 sorcery point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

options.name: Distant Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double the range of the spell. When you cast a spell that has a range of touch, you can spend 1 sorcery point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.

options.name: Empowered Spell
options.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls. You can use Empowered Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

options.name: Extended Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.

options.name: Heightened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend 3 sorcery points to give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

options.name: Quickened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, you can spend 2 sorcery points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

options.name: Subtle Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

options.name: Twinned Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell (1 sorcery point if the spell is a cantrip). To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell's current level. For example, magic missile and scorching ray aren't eligible, but ray of frost and chromatic orb are.

A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.
